---
title: "Volt Typhoon Analysis"
---

## Overview

This website presents an overview of Volt Typhoon activity, mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics, detection sources, and mitigations.

## Activity Timeline

This timeline shows how observed activity develops over time.

<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_activity_timeline.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>

## Detection Sources

This chart shows the ATT&CK detection data components most frequently associated with techniques attributed to Volt Typhoon. Each bar represents the number of ATT&CK detection references that rely on a specific telemetry source, such as process creation events, command execution logs, network traffic content, or user authentication records. Higher values indicate that a particular data source is relevant to detecting a larger portion of Volt Typhoon's documented techniques.
<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_detection_sources.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>
So what?<br>
 - Volt Typhoon frequently uses legitimate administrative tools and valid credentials, making behavior-based monitoring more valuable than traditional antivirus signatures.<br>
 - The telemetry most useful for detecting Volt Typhoon is generated by normal user and system activity, i.e.
 <span data-bs-toggle="tooltip"
      data-bs-placement="top"
      title="Records when a program starts running on a computer">
 process creation
 </span>
 , <span data-bs-toggle="tooltip"
      data-bs-placement="top"
      title="Records commands entered by a user or script into a system shell (such as Command Prompt or PowerShell)">
command execution 
</span>
 , <span data-bs-toggle="tooltip"
      data-bs-placement="top"
      title="Records successful and failed login attempts, account use, and credential activity.">
authentication events
</span>
, and <span data-bs-toggle="tooltip"
      data-bs-placement="top"
      title="Records network connections and data exchanged between devices.">
network communications
</span>
.<br>
 - Effective defense against Volt Typhoon depends less on signature-based malware detection and more on maintaining comprehensive visibility into endpoint, authentication, and network behavior.<br>

## Mitigation Coverage

This chart shows which ATT&CK techniques have stronger or weaker mitigation coverage.

<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_mitigation_coverage.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>

## Tactic Coverage Heatmap

This heatmap shows how Volt Typhoon techniques are distributed across tactics.

<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_tactic_coverage_heatmap.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>


<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
  var tooltipTriggerList = [].slice.call(
    document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="tooltip"]')
  );
  tooltipTriggerList.map(function (tooltipTriggerEl) {
    return new bootstrap.Tooltip(tooltipTriggerEl);
  });
});
</script>
